description: validate a config_infer override and preview estimated VRAM/wall-time/disk without launching GPU diffusion inference
rules: thin wrapper: runs run_rflow_ct.py --preflight-only — the same preflight the generate perk runs every invocation, stopped before scripts.inference launches

checks: config-schema bounds, anatomy_list names against label_dict, body_region in the supported set, controllable_anatomy_size constraints, output_size/spacing contracts, body-region-aware x/y FOV minimums, mask-candidate dataset presence, CUDA availability, and an estimated peak VRAM / wall-time
outputs preflight.json (estimated_cost + cuda + rendered_infer_config, or preflight_errors) under record_store; never launches inference and never mutates the upstream clone
NV_GENERATE_ROOT must point at a cloned NV-Generate-CTMR repo; missing CUDA or datasets are reported as preflight errors rather than crashing
CONFIG_INFER override keys are restricted (num_output_samples, body_region, anatomy_list, controllable_anatomy_size, output_size, spacing, ...); pass 'default' for the upstream config verbatim
usage: Set CONFIG_INFER (override JSON path or 'default') and NV_GENERATE_ROOT, optionally VERSION (rflow-ct or ddpm-ct). Output: preflight.json with the rendered config, estimated cost, and CUDA report — use it to catch config typos and confirm a run will fit before paying for the generate perk.
limitation: Cost estimates are coarse rflow-ct-on-RTX-6000-Ada calibrations meant to gate 'this won't fit' cases, not to schedule cluster jobs. Does not produce CT volumes — use the generate perk for that.
